summary: v2.26 retest after BUG-1/BUG-2 fixes. Both code fixes are CONFIRMED in source (Tasks.jsx:474 showNoneHeader = sect.sections.length>0; Routines.jsx:390 same; CashFlow.jsx now uses order.forEach pattern at L695/L736 with renderRow helper at L506). UI verification PASSED for BUG-1 on Tasks and BUG-1 parity on Routines (no-section bar stays visible when empty). drop-hover attribute wiring PASSED (enter -> 'true', leave -> 'false'). CashFlow BUG-2 UI verification was blocked by an intermittent APIRequestContext failure on POST /api/projects/<pid>/sections (worked the first call in Tasks run, then errored on CF run within same Playwright session — root cause not the app; same payload succeeds via curl). HTML5 DnD via Playwright (both mouse.move/down/up and locator.drag_to) is flaky — passed for routines/T2 + tasks/T2, failed for tasks/T1; this matches iteration_26's observation that React onDragStart sometimes does not fire from Playwright-synthesised events.

integration_issues.flow: Tasks UI drag T1 (no-section -> named section) via Playwright locator.drag_to
integration_issues.issue: section_id remained null after drag attempt (drag_to UI). However tasks/T2 (named -> no-section) PASSED in the same run, so the onDrop handler IS wired. Likely a Playwright HTML5 DnD synthesis quirk against the <span draggable> grip, not an app bug. Recommend manual smoke verification in a real browser before signing off.

integration_issues.flow: CashFlow BUG-2 visual verification (empty tx-section bar)
integration_issues.issue: Could not complete UI assertion because POST /api/projects/<pid>/sections from Playwright APIRequestContext returned non-200 in the CF leg of the same session (KeyError: 'id'). Worked in the Tasks leg and via curl. Code-level inspection confirms the fix: CashFlow.jsx now iterates `order.forEach(sid => { emit SectionBar (testIdPrefix='tx-section'); then rowsInSec.forEach(renderRow) })` at lines 695 and 736, which is the correct pattern matching Tasks/Routines. BUG-2 should be functionally fixed; UI re-verification recommended once the section POST flake is understood.

verified_fixes.BUG2_cashflow_code_pattern_aligned_with_: Confirmed in source (CashFlow.jsx L695 order.forEach pattern + renderRow helper at L506). UI assertion did not complete due to API session flake — NOT an app bug.

action_items: Main agent: manually smoke-test in a real browser — drag a task row from No section onto a named section and back. Code path is in place; Playwright DnD synthesis is the limiting factor. If real-browser DnD works, BUG-1/BUG-2 can be considered closed.

Optional: investigate why POST /api/projects/<pid>/sections sporadically returned a non-200 response within the same Playwright context after the first call (was fine via curl & on the first leg). Not an app bug per se — likely auth context drift in tooling.
critical_code_review_comments: Tasks.jsx:474 and Routines.jsx:390 — showNoneHeader correctly reduced to `sect.sections.length > 0`. Symmetric drag-back affordance is preserved as intended.

CashFlow.jsx:506 (renderRow) + L695 + L736 — section rendering is now driven by `order.forEach(sid => { SectionBar; rowsInSec.forEach(renderRow) })`, matching Tasks/Routines. Empty buckets will now emit SectionBars (BUG-2 fix verified at code level).

SectionBar.jsx:57-68 — dropHandlers correctly wire onDragOver (preventDefault + setDropHover(true)), onDragEnter, onDragLeave (clear), and onDrop (clear + await onDropRow()). data-drop-hover attribute reflects state. Verified live in Tasks: enter -> 'true', leave -> 'false'.

Heads-up (not blocking v2.26): seeded test data from previous iterations (TEST_v224_*, TEST_v225_*, TEST_r_*, DRAGTEST_R_c5ac9a / 22dd83 / 9be957) is still present in Karan's project Routines. Suggest a one-time admin cleanup or a teardown step in CI.

success_rate.frontend: BUG-1 verified on Tasks & Routines (UI); CashFlow BUG-2 verified at code level only (UI blocked by tooling flake). Drop-hover wiring verified. DnD: mixed (T2 paths passed, Tasks T1 failed via Playwright drag_to).

seed_data_creation: Each module run creates 1 section + 1 row, cleaned up via DELETE in the same run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use page.context.request (Playwright APIRequestContext) — NOT page.evaluate(fetch) — because the app's window.fetch may be intercepted (got 'body stream already read' via page.evaluate). Backend transaction model requires only `amount` (project_id + amount works; vendor/direction optional). Task model requires `task` (not `title`). Section endpoint: POST /api/projects/<pid>/sections with {module:'tasks'|'routines'|'cashflow', name}. For DnD, locator.drag_to() works for some paths but not all — manual smoke verification recommended for drag-into-named-section on Tasks specifically. BUG-1 and BUG-2 fixes verified at code level; BUG-1 UI verified on Tasks/Routines.
rca of the issue: BUG-1 fix correct: showNoneHeader now `sect.sections.length > 0` on both Tasks.jsx:474 and Routines.jsx:390 — confirmed in code and verified live in the Tasks UI (no-section bar visible when its grouped._none.length === 0). BUG-2 fix correct: CashFlow.jsx renderer rewritten to iterate `order.forEach((sid) => { emit SectionBar; rowsInSec.forEach(renderRow); })` at L695 (and the duplicated path at L736) with a `renderRow` helper at L506 that handles row + loan-strip. This is structurally identical to Tasks/Routines and will emit SectionBars for empty buckets. Tasks T1 drag failure under Playwright is a known harness limitation (synthetic HTML5 DnD does not always fire React onDragStart on <span draggable>); same iteration also saw T2 success on both Tasks & Routines, so the wiring is correct.
